evals.prompt: I'm building a Power BI report to track donations for my nonprofit. We have a Donations fact table with columns: DonationID, DonorID, DonationDate, Amount, ProgramArea (like 'Education', 'Healthcare', 'Agriculture'). I also have a Donors dimension table with DonorID, DonorName, DonorType ('Individual', 'Foundation', 'Corporate'), Country, and FirstDonationDate. I need DAX measures for: (1) Total Donations, (2) Year-over-Year donation growth percentage, (3) Donor retention rate (donors who gave this year AND last year divided by donors who gave last year), and (4) a running total of donations within the current year. Also, do I need a separate date table or can I just use DonationDate directly?
evals.expected_output: Complete DAX measures for all 4 calculations using VAR/RETURN, explanation of why a separate date table is required, and the date table creation pattern. Should explain filter context for each measure.
expectations: Provides a complete Total Donations measure using SUM or SUMX
Provides a YoY growth percentage measure using SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR or DATEADD
Provides a donor retention rate measure using CALCULATE with date filters and DISTINCTCOUNT
Provides a running total / cumulative measure within the fiscal year
Explains that a separate date table is required for time intelligence to work
Shows how to create a date table (either DAX CALENDAR or Power Query)
Uses VAR/RETURN pattern in at least 2 of the measures
Mentions marking the table as a date table

evals.prompt: I exported survey data from KoboToolbox as a CSV. It has about 80 columns -- each column is a survey question like 'Q1_age', 'Q2_gender', 'Q3_education_level', 'Q4_water_source', all the way to Q80. Each row is one respondent (about 2000 respondents). Some cells have multiple answers separated by semicolons (like 'Well;River;Tap' for water source). A bunch of cells are empty where people skipped questions. I need to get this into Power BI in a way that actually performs well and lets me cross-filter between questions. What's the best approach? Can you give me the Power Query M code to transform this?
evals.expected_output: Recommends unpivoting the wide table into a star schema. Provides M code for unpivoting, splitting multi-select responses, creating dimension tables. Explains why wide tables kill Power BI performance.
expectations: Recommends unpivoting the wide format into a tall/narrow structure
Explains why wide tables (80+ columns) hurt VertiPaq performance
Provides M code for the unpivot transformation
Addresses multi-select (semicolon-separated) responses with a split pattern
Recommends creating a star schema with fact and dimension tables
Addresses null/empty handling for skipped questions
Mentions that cross-filtering requires the normalized structure

evals.prompt: My Power BI report takes about 45 seconds to load a single page. The model is about 800MB in Import mode with 12 million rows in the main fact table. I have about 25 visuals on the page including 8 card visuals, a couple of matrices, some bar charts, and a map. Several of my DAX measures use FILTER(ALL(...)) patterns. The fact table has a lot of text columns like full addresses, descriptions, and notes. What's going wrong and how do I fix it?
evals.expected_output: Systematic diagnosis covering: too many visuals per page, high-cardinality text columns bloating VertiPaq, FILTER(ALL()) anti-pattern in DAX, and specific remediation steps for each issue.
expectations: Identifies too many visuals on one page (25 is excessive, recommend under 15-20)
Identifies high-cardinality text columns (addresses, descriptions, notes) as VertiPaq compression killers
Recommends removing or moving text columns to a separate table
Identifies FILTER(ALL(...)) as a DAX anti-pattern and recommends CALCULATE with direct predicates
Recommends using Performance Analyzer to identify the slowest visuals
Suggests splitting the page into multiple pages with drill-through
Mentions DAX Studio or VertiPaq Analyzer for deeper diagnosis

evals.prompt: I want to enable Copilot in Power BI for my organization. We currently have Power BI Pro licenses for everyone. What do I need to do? What's the minimum capacity required?
evals.expected_output: Explains that Copilot requires F2+ Fabric capacity (not Pro), provides step-by-step activation, and notes that Pro alone is insufficient.
expectations: States that Copilot requires Fabric capacity, minimum F2 SKU
Explains that Pro licenses alone do NOT support Copilot
Provides steps to enable Copilot in the Admin Portal tenant settings
Mentions assigning a workspace to the Fabric capacity
Notes that Copilot is enabled by default for tenants with eligible capacity since September 2025
Mentions the data residency / geographic processing toggle as a compliance consideration

evals.prompt: I have a Sales fact table and a separate Budget table. Both have a Date column but at different grains -- Sales is daily, Budget is monthly. I don't want to create a physical relationship between them because it would be many-to-many and messy. How can I compare actual sales vs. budget in the same visual?
evals.expected_output: Recommends TREATAS to create a virtual relationship between the tables through the shared date dimension, with complete DAX code.
expectations: Recommends TREATAS as the solution for virtual relationships
Provides complete DAX measure code for budget vs. actual comparison
Explains why a physical relationship would be problematic (different grains, many-to-many ambiguity)
Shows how TREATAS applies filter context from one table to another
Recommends both tables relate to a shared Calendar dimension table

evals.prompt: I need to match customer names between two systems but the names are slightly different -- one has 'ABC Corporation' and the other has 'ABC Corp.' or 'A.B.C. Corporation'. How can I handle this in Power Query?
evals.expected_output: Recommends fuzzy matching with Table.FuzzyJoin or Table.FuzzyNestedJoin, explains threshold tuning and transformation tables for known synonyms.
expectations: Recommends Table.FuzzyJoin or Table.FuzzyNestedJoin as the solution
Provides M code with the fuzzy join syntax including threshold parameter
Explains the threshold parameter (0 to 1, higher = stricter)
Mentions the TransformationTable option for known synonyms like Corp/Corporation
Provides the UI path as an alternative (Merge Queries > Use fuzzy matching checkbox)

evals.prompt: We're currently on Power BI Premium P2 capacity. I heard P-SKUs are being retired. What's happening and what do we need to do?
evals.expected_output: Explains P-SKU retirement timeline and migration path to Fabric F-SKUs.
expectations: States that P-SKUs can no longer be purchased new (since July 2024)
States that existing P-SKU renewals ended February 2025
States the full retirement deadline is December 2026
Recommends migrating to Fabric F-SKUs
Notes that all P-SKU features are available on equivalent F-SKUs
Mentions current Pro pricing ($14/user/mo) or PPU pricing ($24/user/mo) as context

evals.prompt: I have 15 measures in my model (Revenue, Cost, Profit, Units, etc.) and I need YTD, Prior Year, and YoY% versions of each. That's 45 measures to create and maintain. Is there a better way?
evals.expected_output: Recommends calculation groups as the solution, explains SELECTEDMEASURE() pattern, and notes that Tabular Editor is required to create them.
expectations: Recommends calculation groups as the solution to measure proliferation
Explains the SELECTEDMEASURE() function that applies to any measure dynamically
Notes that calculation groups must be created in Tabular Editor (not Power BI Desktop UI)
Provides example calculation items for Current, YTD, Prior Year, and YoY%
Explains how the user would use the calculation group in a visual (add to rows/columns)
